title: Phase 6: Content Updates from Audit
description:
## Goal
Apply all game design content updates identified in the audit. This phase updates item/ability/school/background descriptions and effect data in JSON files to match the new game rules.

## Context
The player (game designer) has audited their items and identified specific text and mechanical updates. These must be applied to the JSON data files and their corresponding effects arrays. All pipeline infrastructure should be in place from Phases 1-5.

## EXACT UPDATE TEXT (preserve these exactly)

### Background Updates

**Cartographer's Guild** (keeperBackgrounds.json, key: "cartographer")
- Old benefit: "The first time you begin a Dungeon Crawl (draw a ♠) each month, you automatically gain +15 Ink Drops for mapping the territory."
- New benefit: "For each dungeon room card you add to your active quests gain 15 ink drops"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_DRAFTED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "GRANT_RESOURCE", resource: "inkDrops", value: 15 } }

### School Updates

**Evocation** (schoolBenefits.json, key: "Evocation")
- Old benefit: "Once per month, when battling a monster in a dungeon, you may choose to defeat it quickly by reading a short story or novella instead of a novel."
- New benefit: "Once per month you may use one book to complete two quests, regardless of prompt"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "complete_two_quests_one_book" }, cooldown: "monthly" }

**Enchantment** (schoolBenefits.json, key: "Enchantment")
- Old benefit: "When you befriend a monster in a dungeon, you earn 1.5x the base XP for that quest."
- New benefit: "When you face a monster in a dungeon, you earn 1.5x XP"
- Effect update: Change condition from { encounterAction: "befriend" } to remove encounterAction restriction (any monster encounter)
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl", encounterType: "Monster" }, modifier: { type: "MULTIPLY", resource: "xp", value: 1.5 } }

**Conjuration** (schoolBenefits.json, key: "Conjuration")
- Old benefit: "When you complete a quest while your Familiar slot is equipped, you gain an additional +5 Ink Drops for each quest."
- New benefit: "Gain an additional familiar slot"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_STATE_LOAD", modifier: { type: "UNLOCK_SLOT", slotType: "familiar" } }

### Reward Item Updates

**Librarian's Quill** (allItems.json, key: "Librarian's Quill")
- Old bonus: "Earn a permanent +2 Paper Scraps bonus for every book you journal about after finishing."
- New bonus: "An additional +2 paper scraps for each journal entry"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 2 } }
- Note: rewardModifier.paperScraps: 2 stays for backward compat with existing quests

**Scepter of Knowledge** (allItems.json, key: "The Scepter of Knowledge")
- Old bonus: "Once per month, you may switch the genre of any quest you roll to Non-Fiction."
- New bonus: "Once per month you may ignore the prompt and read a non-fiction book instead"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "ignore_prompt_read_nonfiction" }, cooldown: "monthly" }

**Celestial Koi Fish** (allItems.json, key: "Celestial Koi Fish")
- Old bonus: "Once per month, you may use this familiar's insight to switch a genre-based quest to its opposing genre."
- New bonus: "An additional +10XP for books with celestial motif or a nights sky on the cover"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "xp", value: 10 } }
- Note: The condition is subjective (celestial motif / night sky cover), so this would be a player-selected buff card effect, not auto-apply. Add the effect but keep it as manual selection. Update rewardModifier to { xp: 10 } and passiveRewardModifier to { xp: 5 }.
- Remove excludeFromQuestBonuses: true since it now has a quest bonus

### School Mastery Ability Updates

**Flicker of Prophecy** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Flicker of Prophecy")
- Old benefit: "When rolling a d6 for a Genre Quest, you may choose to treat the result as one number higher or lower"
- New benefit: "When pulling genre quests, pull 3 quests and select the one you most want to read"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "pull_extra_genre_quest" }, description: "Pull 3 genre quests and select 1" }

**Master of Fates** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Master of Fates")
- Old benefit: "Once per month, when establishing your Monthly Quest Pool, you may draw two additional cards."
- New benefit: "When drawing any quest, draw one additional card from each quest category. Then pick any cards you want up to your pre-determined quest pool number"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "draw_extra_from_each_category" }, description: "Draw 1 extra card from each quest category, pick up to pool number" }

**Quick Shot** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Quick Shot")
- Old benefit: "The benefit of Evocation may now be used to complete any single dungeon room challenge with a short story"
- New benefit: "Any dungeon room and encounter can be completed by the same book, regardless of prompt"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "same_book_room_and_encounter" } }

**Concussive Blast** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Concussive Blast")
- Old benefit: "When you use your Evocation benefit to complete a dungeon room challenge, the blast of power also completes a second prompt in the same room"
- New benefit: "When completing a quest you may choose to also complete the quest to the left or right of the quest card with the same book"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "complete_adjacent_quest" } }

**Silver Tongue** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Silver Tongue")
- Old benefit: "When you complete a Side Quest, you gain an additional +5 Paper Scraps"
- New benefit: "When you complete any quest, you gain an additional +5 Paper Scraps, representing your knack for getting a little extra out of every interaction"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 5 } }
- Note: Condition changed from side-quest-only to ANY quest

**Irresistible Charm** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Irresistible Charm")
- Old benefit: "Once per month, you may choose to automatically succeed at befriending a monster"
- New benefit: "You can choose to automatically complete any encounter prompt with the same book as the dungeon room, who would dare attack you?"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "auto_complete_encounter" } }
- Note: No longer monthly cooldown — always available

**Echo Chamber** (masteryAbilities.json, key: "Echo Chamber")
- Old benefit: "You permanently unlock an additional Familiar slot on top of any Familiar slots you have already unlocked."
- New benefit: "You have mastered the art of magical connection. The bonuses granted by each familiar you have equipped are doubled"
- Effect: { trigger: "ON_STATE_LOAD", modifier: { type: "MULTIPLY", target: "familiar_bonuses", value: 2 } }

## Process
1. Update each JSON file with new benefit text and effects arrays
2. Run node scripts/generate-data.js
3. Run tests to verify nothing breaks
4. Verify the character sheet loads correctly in browser

## Key Files
- MODIFIED: assets/data/keeperBackgrounds.json
- MODIFIED: assets/data/schoolBenefits.json
- MODIFIED: assets/data/masteryAbilities.json
- MODIFIED: assets/data/allItems.json

## Success Criteria
- All benefit text matches the exact update text above
- All effects arrays are valid per the effect schema
- generate-data.js runs without errors
- All tests pass
- Character sheet loads and displays updated text

title: Page-count-aware reward modifiers for items

comments.author: tshardey
comments.text:
Three items currently have page-count-dependent bonuses described in their bonus text but the RewardCalculator applies them unconditionally (flat inkDrops/multiplier) regardless of actual page count:

1. The Bookwyrm's Scale - "For every book over 500 pages, gain a +10 Ink Drop bonus" / passive +5. Currently: flat rewardModifier { inkDrops: 10 }. Should: only apply when pageCountEffective >= 500.

2. Tome of Potential - "Earn a x3 Ink Drop bonus for books over 400 pages" / passive x1.5. Currently: flat rewardModifier { inkDropsMultiplier: 3 }. Should: only apply when pageCountEffective >= 400.

3. Page Sprite - "Earn a x2 Ink Drop bonus on any book under 300 pages" / passive x1.5. Currently: flat rewardModifier { inkDropsMultiplier: 2 }. Should: only apply when pageCountEffective < 300.

Implementation plan:
- Add structured page-count condition fields to these items in assets/data/allItems.json (e.g. pageCondition: { min: 500 } or pageCondition: { max: 299 }). Also add passivePageCondition where the passive threshold differs.
- Run node scripts/generate-data.js after updating JSON.
- Update RewardCalculator._getModifier() or applyModifiers() to accept the quest pageCountEffective (falling back to pageCountRaw) and skip/zero-out modifiers when the page condition is not met.
- Thread quest page count through calculateFinalRewards() - the quest object is already passed in options, so read quest.pageCountEffective ?? quest.pageCountRaw there.
- If pageCountEffective is null/undefined (legacy quest with no page data), the item should NOT apply its bonus (conservative: require known page count for page-gated rewards).
- Update the reward receipt to note when a page-count item was skipped (e.g. "Bookwyrm's Scale: skipped (page count unknown)" or "Bookwyrm's Scale: skipped (320 pages < 500 threshold)").
- Consider future extensibility: a pageFormula field for items like "+15 Ink Drops for every 100 pages over 300" (scaling bonuses). Not required for current items but the schema should allow it.
- Add tests in tests/ covering: item applied when threshold met, item skipped when not met, item skipped when page count unknown, passive vs active thresholds.

title: Phase 2: Wire ON_QUEST_COMPLETED trigger into ModifierPipeline
description:
## Goal
Wire the ModifierPipeline into the quest completion flow so that background, school, and mastery ability effects auto-apply when quests are completed. This replaces the hardcoded Biblioslinker and Enchantment branches in RewardCalculator.js.

## Context
After Phase 1, we have the ModifierPipeline engine, EffectRegistry, and structured effects in JSON. Currently, quest completion rewards flow through:
1. BaseQuestHandler.completeActiveQuest() calls RewardCalculator.getBaseRewards()
2. Then RewardCalculator.applyModifiers() for player-selected buff cards
3. Then RewardCalculator.applyBackgroundBonuses() — only handles Biblioslinker (hardcoded)
4. Then RewardCalculator.applySchoolBonuses() — only handles Enchantment (hardcoded)

After this phase, the flow becomes:
1. RewardCalculator.getBaseRewards() (unchanged)
2. ModifierPipeline.resolve(ON_QUEST_COMPLETED, payload, activeEffects, baseReward)
   - This applies ALL auto-apply effects: background, school, mastery abilities
   - Then applies player-selected buff cards (existing rewardModifier system)
   - Resolution order: flat → multiplier → prevent

## What to Change

### 1. Create QuestCompletedPayload
In TriggerPayload.js, implement the factory for quest completion context:
```js
{
  questType: 'dungeon_crawl' | 'genre_quest' | 'side_quest' | 'extra_credit',
  prompt: string,
  isEncounter: boolean,
  encounterName: string,
  encounterType: 'Monster' | 'Friendly Creature' | 'Familiar' | null,
  isBefriend: boolean,
  roomNumber: string | null,
  genre: string | null,
  pageCount: number | null,
  bookId: string | null
}
```

Map from existing quest type strings:
- '♠ Dungeon Crawl' → 'dungeon_crawl'
- '♥ Organize the Stacks' → 'genre_quest'
- '♣ Side Quest' → 'side_quest'
- '⭐ Extra Credit' → 'extra_credit'

### 2. Update RewardCalculator.calculateFinalRewards
Replace the sequential applyModifiers → applyBackgroundBonuses → applySchoolBonuses flow with a single ModifierPipeline.resolve() call. The pipeline should:
1. Get auto-apply effects from EffectRegistry for ON_QUEST_COMPLETED
2. Get player-selected buff card effects (convert existing [Item]/[Buff]/[Background] prefixed names to modifier objects)
3. Merge both into the resolution pipeline
4. Return Reward with complete receipt

### 3. Update BaseQuestHandler.completeActiveQuest
This method recalculates rewards from scratch. Update it to use the pipeline instead of calling applyBackgroundBonuses/applySchoolBonuses directly. It needs access to stateAdapter (or equivalent) to query EffectRegistry.

### 4. Remove hardcoded branches
- Remove applyBackgroundBonuses() from RewardCalculator — Biblioslinker dungeon paper scraps now handled by effect: { trigger: ON_QUEST_COMPLETED, condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: ADD_FLAT, resource: "paperScraps", value: 10 } }
- Remove applySchoolBonuses() from RewardCalculator — Enchantment 1.5x XP now handled by effect: { trigger: ON_QUEST_COMPLETED, condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl", encounterType: "Monster" }, modifier: { type: MULTIPLY, resource: "xp", value: 1.5 } }
- Note: Archivist, Prophet, Cartographer background bonuses were previously manual-only via [Background] buff card prefix. They now have auto-apply effects. However, the [Background] buff card entries should still work for backward compat (players who have quests in progress with those buffs selected).

### 5. Auto-apply effects for ON_QUEST_COMPLETED
These should now automatically apply without player buff card selection:
- Enchantment: 1.5x XP for monster encounters in dungeons
- Biblioslinker: +10 Paper Scraps for dungeon crawl completion
- Silver Tongue (mastery): +5 Paper Scraps on any quest completion
- Alchemic Focus (mastery): +5 XP for extra credit quests
- Conjuration school: +5 Ink Drops per quest when familiar is equipped

### 6. Player-selected buff cards continue working
Items like Librarian's Compass, Dragon Fang, etc. that have subjective conditions ("books by a new-to-you author", "books with dragons") continue to use the existing buff card selection UI and rewardModifier/passiveRewardModifier system. The pipeline incorporates these as manual-select effects alongside auto-apply effects.

### 7. Update tests
- Update RewardCalculator.test.js — tests for applyBackgroundBonuses and applySchoolBonuses should verify the same behavior now comes from the pipeline
- Update RewardCalculatorReceipts.test.js — receipt format should still show modifier sources
- Add new tests for auto-apply behaviors: Enchantment auto-applies without buff card selection, Biblioslinker auto-applies, Silver Tongue auto-applies
- ALL existing tests must still pass

## Key Files
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/RewardCalculator.js (remove hardcoded branches, delegate to pipeline)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js (integration)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/TriggerPayload.js (QuestCompletedPayload)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/EffectRegistry.js (integration with stateAdapter)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/quest-handlers/BaseQuestHandler.js (use pipeline)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/QuestController.js (pass pipeline/stateAdapter)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet.js (wire pipeline into dependencies)
- MODIFIED: tests/RewardCalculator.test.js
- MODIFIED: tests/RewardCalculatorReceipts.test.js

## Success Criteria
- Background bonuses auto-apply without buff card selection
- School bonuses auto-apply without buff card selection
- Mastery ability bonuses auto-apply without buff card selection
- Existing buff card system still works for subjective items
- Receipt shows all applied modifiers with sources
- All existing tests pass
- New auto-apply behavior tests pass
close_reason: Phase 2: ON_QUEST_COMPLETED wired through ModifierPipeline + EffectRegistry; RewardCalculator/BaseQuestHandler/QuestController/QuestService updated; tests pass

title: Phase 4: Wire ON_QUEST_DRAFTED and ON_MONTH_START triggers
description:
## Goal
Wire the ModifierPipeline into quest drafting (adding quests to active pool) and month start flows. This handles Cartographer's Guild instant resource grant and quest pool modification abilities.

## Context
Two triggers to implement:

ON_QUEST_DRAFTED — fires when a quest card is added to active quests:
- Cartographer's Guild: gain 15 Ink Drops when adding a dungeon room card

ON_MONTH_START — fires when starting a new month:
- Divination school: reroll quest die (monthly cooldown)
- Lantern of Foresight (item): reroll prompt or die (monthly cooldown)
- Flicker of Prophecy (mastery): pull 3 genre quests, select 1
- Master of Fates (mastery): draw 1 extra card from each quest category, pick up to pool size
- Grove Tender: force "The Soaking in Nature" atmospheric buff active
- Gilded Painting, Garden Gnome, Mystical Moth: add atmospheric buffs

## What to Change

### 1. ON_QUEST_DRAFTED trigger
Currently there is no automated resource grant when adding quests. The Cartographer's Guild background benefit says "first time you begin a Dungeon Crawl each month, you gain +15 Ink Drops" but the new text from the audit says "For each dungeon room card you add to your active quests gain 15 ink drops."

Implementation:
- In QuestController, when a quest is added to active assignments, fire ON_QUEST_DRAFTED through the pipeline
- The payload includes: { questType, roomNumber }
- Cartographer effect: { trigger: "ON_QUEST_DRAFTED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "GRANT_RESOURCE", resource: "inkDrops", value: 15 } }
- GRANT_RESOURCE effects call updateCurrency immediately (they don't modify a reward calc, they grant directly)
- Show a toast notification: "Cartographer's Guild: +15 Ink Drops"

### 2. ON_MONTH_START trigger — Quest pool modifications
These abilities modify how quest drawing works. They don't add flat resources — they change the draw/selection process.

Divination school: "Roll 2 dice instead of 1 for a Monthly Quest, choose which result"
- This modifies the genre quest deck controller's draw behavior
- Effect type: ACTIVATE with action "extra_die_roll"

Flicker of Prophecy (mastery): "When pulling genre quests, pull 3 quests and select the one you most want to read"
- Modifies GenreQuestDeckController to draw 3 instead of 1
- Effect type: ACTIVATE with action "pull_extra_genre_quest"

Master of Fates (mastery): "When drawing any quest, draw one additional card from each quest category. Then pick any cards you want up to your pre-determined quest pool number"
- Modifies ALL deck controllers to draw +1 extra
- Effect type: ACTIVATE with action "draw_extra_from_each_category"

Lantern of Foresight (item): "Re-roll a prompt or a die roll"
- Monthly cooldown activated ability
- Effect type: ACTIVATE with action "reroll"

For these, the pipeline's role is to DETECT which abilities are active at month start. The deck controllers then check the pipeline/registry for active quest-pool-modifying effects and adjust their draw counts accordingly.

### 3. ON_MONTH_START trigger — Atmospheric buff effects
- Grove Tender: force "The Soaking in Nature" buff active (currently hardcoded in BuffController.js and EndOfMonthController.js)
- Gilded Painting / Garden Gnome / Mystical Moth: these add atmospheric buffs (currently handled by atmosphericReward/atmosphericStickerSlug fields in allItems.json)

For these, check if the pipeline should formalize the atmospheric buff system or if the existing atmosphericReward fields are sufficient. The existing system works; the pipeline should at least handle Grove Tender's forced buff through the effect system instead of hardcoded string checks.

### 4. Remove hardcoded Grove Tender checks
Currently BuffController.js and EndOfMonthController.js have hardcoded checks:
- `background === 'groveTender' && buffName === 'The Soaking in Nature'`
Replace with: check EffectRegistry for ON_MONTH_START effects with action "force_atmospheric_buff"

### 5. Update deck controllers
GenreQuestDeckController, DungeonDeckController, SideQuestDeckController, OtherQuestDeckController:
- At month start or when drawing, check EffectRegistry for active quest-pool-modifying effects
- Adjust draw counts based on active abilities (Divination, Flicker of Prophecy, Master of Fates)

### 6. Tests
- Test ON_QUEST_DRAFTED: Cartographer background → dungeon quest added → +15 ink drops
- Test ON_QUEST_DRAFTED: non-Cartographer → no bonus
- Test ON_QUEST_DRAFTED: Cartographer → non-dungeon quest → no bonus
- Test ON_MONTH_START: detect active pool-modifying abilities
- Test Grove Tender forced buff via pipeline instead of hardcoded
- All existing tests pass

## Key Files
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/QuestController.js (fire ON_QUEST_DRAFTED)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/BuffController.js (remove hardcoded Grove Tender)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js (remove hardcoded Grove Tender)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/GenreQuestDeckController.js (quest pool mods)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/DungeonDeckController.js (quest pool mods)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/EffectRegistry.js (GRANT_RESOURCE handling)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js (GRANT_RESOURCE resolution)

## Success Criteria
- Cartographer background auto-grants 15 Ink Drops when dungeon quest added
- Grove Tender forced buff comes from effect data, not hardcoded strings
- Deck controllers query pipeline for active quest-pool abilities
- All existing tests pass

title: Unify quest signature normalization across validator and state adapter
Permanent buffs and class abilities: calculate and apply as appropriate
description:
Ensure permanent buffs and learned mastery (class) abilities are calculated and applied in gameplay, not only displayed.

## Current gaps
- **Permanent bonuses** (assets/data/permanentBonuses.json): e.g. Novice's Focus, Focused Atmosphere, Insightful Draw, Atmospheric Forecaster. generalInfoViewModel and ui only surface them for display; RewardCalculator, EndOfMonthController, and quest draw flows do not apply them.
- **Expedition passive-rule-modifier rewards** (seriesCompletionRewards.json): SeriesCompletionService treats these as text-only; no mechanical application.
- **Learned mastery abilities** (assets/data/masteryAbilities.json): AbilityController spends SMP and persists learned abilities; RewardCalculator, quest resolution, Worn Page handling, and familiar bonus calculations do not read learnedAbilities or apply effects.

## Implementation hook points
- **Reward calculation**: RewardCalculator.js — book completion XP (Novice's Focus), atmospheric ink (Focused Atmosphere), side-quest paper (Silver Tongue), external-book XP (Alchemic Focus), familiar bonus +5 (Empowered Bond).
- **Monthly quest setup**: draw logic for extra card / discard one (Insightful Draw), extra atmospheric roll and choose (Atmospheric Forecaster), draw two extra and choose (Master of Fates).
- **Worn Page**: negate once (Ward Against the Shroud), remove all (Grand Dispelling); state/UX for once-per-month use.
- **Familiar slots**: Echo Chamber grants an extra slot; ensure passive slot count includes it.
- **Genre quest d6**: Flicker of Prophecy adjust roll ±1.
- **Expedition passives**: if any expedition stop text implies numeric bonuses (e.g. +20 XP Organize the Stacks), wire into RewardCalculator or document as display-only.

## Scope
- Audit every entry in permanentBonuses.json, expedition passive-rule-modifier stops in seriesCompletionRewards.json, and every ability in masteryAbilities.json.
- For each effect: decide **auto-applied** (code enforces), **manually tracked** (once-per-month with state/UX), or **informational-only** (document).
- Implement auto-applied effects in the correct paths above.
- Add or extend tests for reward math and effect application (e.g. tests/RewardCalculator, SeriesCompletionService, ability application).
acceptance_criteria: Every permanent bonus and learned ability has a disposition (auto-applied, manually tracked, or display-only). Auto-applied effects are wired into RewardCalculator / monthly quest / Worn Page / familiar flows. Once-per-month abilities have state and UX. Tests added or updated for implemented effects.
close_reason: Child beads .1-.5 complete; workflow abilities covered by project-docs/workflow-permanent-abilities-osm-design.md (.5)

title: Model permanent effects as structured capabilities
description:
Create the foundation for tome-of-secrets-osm so permanent effects can be applied consistently across the character sheet.

Context:
- Current data sources are prose-heavy: assets/data/permanentBonuses.json, assets/data/schoolBenefits.json, assets/data/masteryAbilities.json, and assets/data/seriesCompletionRewards.json.
- Today the app mostly displays these effects or surfaces some of them as helper rows, but there is no single mechanical source of truth for what is unlocked, always-on, monthly, manual, or informational-only.
- Relevant current hook points: assets/js/character-sheet/ui.js, assets/js/character-sheet/questDrawHelperDiscovery.js, assets/js/character-sheet/curseHelperDiscovery.js, assets/js/services/RewardCalculator.js, assets/js/services/SeriesCompletionService.js, assets/js/services/SlotService.js, assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js, and assets/js/character-sheet.js.

Work:
- Audit every permanent effect source and encode the disposition of each effect: auto-applied, helper/manual, workflow-specific, or informational-only.
- Introduce a structured resolver/service/module that derives unlocked permanent capabilities from character state (level, selected school, learned abilities, series expedition progress).
- Prefer typed flags/modifiers over repeated string matching for new mechanics. Existing helper discovery can continue to use prose matching for UI, but implementation logic should consume the structured layer.
- Capture effect metadata needed by downstream systems, such as reward deltas, slot bonuses, familiar bonus boosts, end-of-month modifiers, quest draw helpers, curse mitigation helpers, and monthly action flags.
- Document any effects that still cannot be auto-applied because they require new UX or player choice.

Why this matters:
- Without this foundation, each new permanent bonus has to be re-implemented ad hoc in multiple controllers and services, which increases drift between displayed rules and actual gameplay enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: A single structured effect-resolution layer exists or is fully specified for level bonuses, school benefits, learned mastery abilities, and expedition progress. It exposes enough typed information for reward math, helper UI, slot calculations, and monthly/manual interventions without relying on repeated prose parsing. Tests cover the resolver or equivalent typed mapping logic.

title: Auto-apply deterministic permanent reward modifiers
description:
Implement the always-on permanent effects that should be enforced by code rather than left as display-only or manual helper rows.

Primary effects to cover:
- Level bonus: Novice's Focus from assets/data/permanentBonuses.json (+5 XP for books with 300+ pages).
- Level bonus: Focused Atmosphere (+1 to positive atmospheric Ink Drop bonuses).
- School benefit: Conjuration (+5 Ink Drops on quest completion while a Familiar slot is equipped).
- Mastery ability: Silver Tongue (+5 Paper Scraps on Side Quests).
- Mastery ability: Empowered Bond (+5 to the equipped familiar's Ink Drop or Paper Scrap bonus).
- Mastery ability: Echo Chamber (extra Familiar slot on top of other unlocked slots).

Implementation notes:
- Reward calculation currently lives in assets/js/services/RewardCalculator.js and is used by QuestService, BaseQuestHandler, active quest receipt previews, and quest completion flows.
- Book completion XP is currently granted from assets/js/character-sheet.js via RewardCalculator.calculateBookCompletionRewards(1); page count is available on books/quests and may need to be threaded into the completion hook.
- Atmospheric rewards are processed in assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js via RewardCalculator.calculateAtmosphericBuffRewards().
- Familiar slot totals currently involve SlotService plus passive familiar slot state.
- Learned abilities are persisted but not read by reward logic today.

Important constraints:
- Preserve existing receipts and UI messaging where possible, but make sure permanent bonuses show up in receipts when they alter rewards.
- Do not force these effects through the manual quest bonus card picker in ui.js; these are always-on mechanics, not optional per-quest selections.
- Add or update Jest coverage for each implemented effect and for any new helper/service introduced to expose permanent modifiers.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic permanent reward effects are applied mechanically in the appropriate flows, reflected in receipts where applicable, and covered by regression tests. At minimum, long-book XP, atmospheric ink bonuses, side-quest paper bonuses, familiar reward boosts, and passive slot-capacity effects have a documented implementation status.
close_reason: Wired PermanentEffectCapabilities into RewardCalculator (quest, book completion, atmospheric), BaseQuestHandler, Library book XP, inventory Echo Chamber slot budget; added tests.

title: Apply expedition passive bonuses mechanically
description:
Series expedition rewards in assets/data/seriesCompletionRewards.json currently mix typed rewards (currency, item-slot-bonus, familiar-slot-bonus) with passive-rule-modifier prose. SeriesCompletionService displays those passive lines in the Permanent Bonuses section, but does not apply their mechanics.

This task should wire the expedition passives into the correct systems. Audit and implement at least the following known effects:
- Stop 2: Organize the Stacks quests grant +20 XP instead of +15.
- Stop 5: Adventure Journal entries grant +10 Paper Scraps instead of +5.
- Stop 7: All books read for a Series grant +15 Ink Drops instead of +10.
- Stop 10: familiar-slot-bonus is already typed, but its bundled text also promises +10 XP for all Dungeon Rooms; that numeric bonus is not currently applied and should either be implemented or split/normalized.
- Stop 8: Once per month, ignore any single Shroud penalty or Spoon loss. This likely remains helper/manual unless new UX is added, but it should be intentionally classified rather than forgotten.

Relevant code paths:
- assets/js/services/SeriesCompletionService.js
- assets/js/services/RewardCalculator.js
- assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js
- assets/js/character-sheet.js
- series tracking in assets/js/character-sheet/stateAdapter.js

Preferred direction:
- Normalize expedition passives into structured mechanics alongside the broader permanent-effect resolver, rather than relying on reward text string inspection in gameplay code.
- If needed, evolve the expedition reward schema so passive numeric bonuses are represented explicitly instead of embedded only in text.
acceptance_criteria: Numeric expedition passive bonuses are no longer display-only. The applicable series expedition stops change actual reward math or stateful systems, and tests cover each implemented stop. Any remaining prose-only expedition rewards are explicitly documented with their reason for remaining manual/helper-driven.
close_reason: Wired expedition stops 2/5/7/10 into RewardCalculator and series book ink in markBookComplete; tests added. Stop 8 remains helper/manual via dispositions.

title: Extend helper UX for monthly permanent interventions
description:
Use the existing helper infrastructure to support permanent effects that are not good candidates for fully automatic enforcement because they involve player choice, replacement, cancellation, or once-per-month intervention timing.

Effects in scope:
- Level bonuses: Atmospheric Forecaster and Insightful Draw.
- School benefits: Abjuration and Divination.
- Mastery abilities: Ward Against the Shroud, Grand Dispelling, Flicker of Prophecy, Master of Fates.
- Expedition passive: ignore a single Shroud penalty or Spoon loss once per month.

Current state:
- Quest helper discovery already scans some permanent bonuses, schools, abilities, and expedition passives in assets/js/character-sheet/questDrawHelperDiscovery.js.
- Curse helper discovery scans items, buffs, abilities, schools, and expedition passives in assets/js/character-sheet/curseHelperDiscovery.js, but not level permanent bonuses because none currently fit that domain.
- Helper usage state and end-of-month reset live in assets/js/character-sheet/stateAdapter.js and assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js.
- ui.js renders the helper tables/panels and uses source labels from renderComponents.js.

Work:
- Confirm every monthly permanent intervention is either discoverable today or intentionally added.
- Tighten cadence classification where needed (monthly, always, every-2-months, one-time) and make sure usage resets remain correct.
- Improve helper wording/source labels if needed so players understand whether the source is a level bonus, school benefit, mastery ability, or expedition passive.
- Keep manual/helper effects out of the active quest bonus picker unless they truly function as optional reward modifiers during quest completion.
acceptance_criteria: All monthly or choice-driven permanent effects that remain manual are surfaced intentionally in the helper UX with correct cadence, reset behavior, and source labeling. No relevant permanent effect is left display-only by accident. Tests cover helper discovery and state reset behavior for newly supported sources.
close_reason: Curse helpers: Shroud/Spoon expedition passive; Insightful Draw monthly cadence; labels and tests

title: Design workflow support for dungeon and monthly-action abilities
description:
Several permanent school/mastery effects do not fit reward math or the existing helper rows cleanly because they alter dungeon resolution flow, substitute prompts, or enable monthly spend/convert actions. These should not be left implicit.

Effects to cover:
- School benefit: Evocation (once per month short story/novella substitution for monster encounters).
- Mastery abilities: Quick Shot, Concussive Blast, Irresistible Charm.
- School benefit: Transmutation currency conversion.
- Mastery ability: Philosopher's Stone (sacrifice XP for currency once per month).
- Mastery ability: Alchemic Focus (+5 XP for books read outside the monthly quest pool).

Why this is separate:
- These abilities likely require new state, buttons, drawer actions, or dungeon/quest-resolution UI rather than only RewardCalculator changes.
- Some of them may reuse helper infrastructure, but others need explicit workflow affordances so the player can invoke them at the correct point in play.

Expected output:
- A concrete design for where each ability should live in the UI and controller architecture.
- Recommended persistence/reset strategy in stateAdapter/dataValidator/dataMigrator if new state is needed.
- Clear separation between what should be implemented in tome-of-secrets-osm now versus deferred to a follow-up issue if scope is too large.
acceptance_criteria: There is an implementation-ready design, or completed implementation if chosen, for the remaining permanent abilities that require new workflow/UI rather than simple reward math. The design names the state model, user interaction surface, reset behavior, and controller/service touchpoints for each effect.
close_reason: Added project-docs/workflow-permanent-abilities-osm-design.md: monthly workflow state, per-effect UI/state/controller touchpoints, EOM reset, now vs defer scope

title: Phase 3: Wire ON_MONTH_END and ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY triggers
description:
## Goal
Wire the ModifierPipeline into end-of-month processing and journal entry flows. This handles worn page prevention, journal entry rewards, and atmospheric buff calculation through the pipeline.

## Context
Currently EndOfMonthController.js handles three reward flows at end of month:
1. Atmospheric buff ink rewards — via RewardCalculator.calculateAtmosphericBuffRewards()
2. Journal entry paper scraps — via RewardCalculator.calculateJournalEntryRewards() with hardcoded scribe bonus
3. Worn page prevention is NOT automated — Abjuration, Raven Familiar, Chalice of Restoration, and Ward Against the Shroud all have prose-only descriptions and require manual player action

After this phase, the pipeline handles all three flows.

## What to Change

### 1. ON_MONTH_END trigger for worn page prevention
Items/abilities that prevent worn page at end of month:
- Abjuration school: PREVENT worn_page (monthly cooldown)
- Ward Against the Shroud (mastery): PREVENT worn_page (monthly cooldown)
- Raven Familiar (item): PREVENT worn_page (monthly cooldown, or bi-monthly if passive)
- Chalice of Restoration (item): PREVENT worn_page (monthly cooldown, or bi-monthly if passive)

When end of month fires and a worn page penalty would be applied:
1. Pipeline checks for PREVENT effects on ON_MONTH_END
2. If any active PREVENT for worn_page exists with available cooldown, it intercepts
3. Receipt records which effect prevented it
4. Cooldown is consumed

This requires adding cooldown tracking to state (stateAdapter). Add a new storage key for ability/item cooldowns: { abilityId: { lastUsedMonth: number, lastUsedYear: number } }.

### 2. ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY trigger
Currently RewardCalculator.calculateJournalEntryRewards() has a hardcoded check for background === 'scribe' to add +3 Paper Scraps per entry.

After this phase:
- Base journal entry reward: 5 Paper Scraps per entry (from GAME_CONFIG)
- Scribe's Acolyte background: effect { trigger: ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY, modifier: { type: ADD_FLAT, resource: paperScraps, value: 3 } }
- Librarian's Quill (item, if equipped): effect { trigger: ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY, modifier: { type: ADD_FLAT, resource: paperScraps, value: 2 } }
- Golden Pen (item, if equipped): effect { trigger: ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY, modifier: { type: ADD_FLAT, resource: paperScraps, value: 10 } }

Pipeline resolves all active ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY effects and returns total paper scraps per entry.

Note: Librarian's Quill and Golden Pen currently have excludeFromQuestBonuses: true or are in allItems.json with rewardModifier for paperScraps, but they are categorized as "End of month" effects in the audit. They will need effects arrays added to allItems.json.

### 3. Atmospheric buff calculation
This can largely stay as-is for now (RewardCalculator.calculateAtmosphericBuffRewards is already well-structured). However, if Tome-Bound Cat's atmospheric multiplier or Grove Tender's forced buff are relevant at EOM, ensure the pipeline can handle those. The Tome-Bound Cat atmospheric x2 multiplier is currently in the view model, not in RewardCalculator — reconcile this.

### 4. Remove hardcoded scribe bonus
Remove the hardcoded `if (background === 'scribe')` branch from RewardCalculator.calculateJournalEntryRewards(). The Scribe's Acolyte bonus now comes from its effect in keeperBackgrounds.json via the pipeline.

### 5. Add effects to relevant items in allItems.json
Add effects arrays to items that have ON_MONTH_END or ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY triggers:
- Librarian's Quill: add effects: [{ trigger: "ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 2 } }]
- Golden Pen: add effects: [{ trigger: "ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 10 } }]
- Chalice of Restoration: add effects: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_END", modifier: { type: "PREVENT", target: "worn_page" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Raven Familiar: add effects: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_END", modifier: { type: "PREVENT", target: "worn_page" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]

### 6. Update EndOfMonthController.js
- Journal entry rewards: use pipeline instead of RewardCalculator.calculateJournalEntryRewards()
- Worn page: before applying worn page penalty, check pipeline for PREVENT effects
- Remove hardcoded Grove Tender / "The Soaking in Nature" special case where possible (it should come from groveTender's effect data)

### 7. Tests
- Test ON_MONTH_END PREVENT effects (worn page blocked by Abjuration, Raven Familiar, etc.)
- Test ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY with scribe, with Librarian's Quill, with both
- Test cooldown tracking: PREVENT used once, not available again same month
- All existing tests must pass

## Key Files
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/RewardCalculator.js (remove hardcoded scribe bonus)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js (PREVENT resolution)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js (use pipeline)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/CurseController.js (worn page prevention integration)
- MODIFIED: assets/data/allItems.json (add effects to Quill, Pen, Chalice, Raven)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet/storageKeys.js (cooldown tracking key)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet/stateAdapter.js (cooldown methods)
- NEW or MODIFIED: tests for EOM pipeline behavior

## Success Criteria
- Scribe bonus comes from pipeline, not hardcoded
- Librarian's Quill and Golden Pen auto-apply to journal entries when equipped
- Worn page prevention fires automatically when effects are active
- Cooldown tracking prevents double-use in same month
- All existing tests pass
close_reason: Phase 3: ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY + worn page PREVENT wired; effectCooldowns; tests pass

title: Phase 1: TCG Foundation - Effect Schema, ModifierPipeline, EffectRegistry
description:
## Goal
Build the foundational data-driven modifier pipeline with NO behavior changes to existing code. This phase creates the engine and adds structured effect data to JSON files.

## Context
The current reward system has hardcoded branches in RewardCalculator.js for specific backgrounds (Biblioslinker) and schools (Enchantment). Backgrounds (keeperBackgrounds.json), schools (schoolBenefits.json), and mastery abilities (masteryAbilities.json) only have prose descriptions — no structured data the code can use. This phase adds that structured data and builds the engine to consume it.

## What to Build

### 1. Effect Schema (assets/js/services/effectSchema.js)
Define and export constants for the TCG system:

**Triggers:**
- ON_QUEST_COMPLETED, ON_QUEST_DRAFTED, ON_MONTH_END, ON_MONTH_START
- ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY, ON_BOOK_COMPLETED, ON_STATE_LOAD, ON_ACTIVATE

**Modifier types:**
- ADD_FLAT — add a flat amount to a resource
- MULTIPLY — multiply a resource total
- PREVENT — cancel/block a penalty (e.g., worn page)
- GRANT_RESOURCE — immediately grant resources (not part of reward calc)
- UNLOCK_SLOT — unlock a permanent slot/feature
- ACTIVATE — one-time use ability with cooldown

**Resolution order:** ADD_FLAT → MULTIPLY → PREVENT (this matches existing RewardCalculator.applyModifiers pattern)

**Effect object shape:**
```json
{
  "trigger": "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED",
  "condition": { "questType": "dungeon_crawl", "encounterAction": "befriend" },
  "modifier": { "type": "MULTIPLY", "resource": "xp", "value": 1.5 }
}
```

Include a validateEffect(effect) function that checks an effect object has valid trigger, modifier type, etc.

### 2. ModifierPipeline (assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js)
Core engine that:
- Takes a trigger name + payload (quest context, month context, etc.)
- Collects all active effects for that trigger from EffectRegistry
- Evaluates conditions against the payload
- Applies modifiers in resolution order: base → ADD_FLAT → MULTIPLY → PREVENT
- Returns a Reward object (reuse existing Reward class from RewardCalculator.js) with receipt

Key method: `static resolve(trigger, payload, activeEffects, baseReward)`

### 3. EffectRegistry (assets/js/services/EffectRegistry.js)
Gathers all active "cards" and their effects:
- Reads player's current background from state → looks up effects in keeperBackgrounds data
- Reads player's current school from state → looks up effects in schoolBenefits data
- Reads player's learned abilities from state → looks up effects in masteryAbilities data
- Reads player's equipped items from state → looks up effects in allItems data
- Reads player's active temp buffs from state → looks up effects in temporaryBuffs data

Key method: `static getActiveEffects(trigger, stateAdapter, dataModule)` returns array of effect objects with source metadata.

### 4. TriggerPayload (assets/js/services/TriggerPayload.js)
Standardized payload factory for each trigger type:
- QuestCompletedPayload: { questType, prompt, isEncounter, encounterName, encounterType, isBefriend, roomNumber, genre, pageCount, bookId }
- QuestDraftedPayload: { questType, roomNumber }
- MonthEndPayload: { journalEntries, booksCompleted, atmosphericBuffs }
- MonthStartPayload: { questPoolSize }
- JournalEntryPayload: { entryCount }

### 5. Add effects arrays to JSON data files

**keeperBackgrounds.json** — add "effects" array to each background:
- scribe: [{ trigger: "ON_JOURNAL_ENTRY", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 3 } }]
- archivist: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { genre: ["Non-Fiction", "Historical Fiction"] }, modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "inkDrops", value: 10 } }]
- cartographer: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_DRAFTED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "GRANT_RESOURCE", resource: "inkDrops", value: 15 } }]
- prophet: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { hasTag: ["religious", "spiritual", "mythological"] }, modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "inkDrops", value: 15 } }]
- biblioslinker: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 10 } }]
- groveTender: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "force_atmospheric_buff", buffName: "The Soaking in Nature" } }]

**schoolBenefits.json** — add "effects" array to each school:
- Abjuration: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_END", modifier: { type: "PREVENT", target: "worn_page" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Divination: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "reroll_quest_die" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Evocation: [{ trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "complete_two_quests_one_book" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Enchantment: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl", encounterType: "Monster" }, modifier: { type: "MULTIPLY", resource: "xp", value: 1.5 } }]
- Conjuration: [{ trigger: "ON_STATE_LOAD", modifier: { type: "UNLOCK_SLOT", slotType: "familiar" } }]
- Transmutation: [{ trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "transmute_currency" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]

**masteryAbilities.json** — add "effects" array to each ability:
- Ward Against the Shroud: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_END", modifier: { type: "PREVENT", target: "worn_page" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Grand Dispelling: [{ trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "remove_all_worn_pages" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]
- Flicker of Prophecy: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "pull_extra_genre_quest" }, description: "Pull 3 genre quests and select 1" }]
- Master of Fates: [{ trigger: "ON_MONTH_START", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "draw_extra_from_each_category" }, description: "Draw 1 extra card from each quest category, pick up to pool number" }]
- Quick Shot: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl" }, modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "same_book_room_and_encounter" }, description: "Any dungeon room and encounter can be completed by the same book" }]
- Concussive Blast: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "complete_adjacent_quest" }, description: "May also complete quest to left or right with same book" }]
- Silver Tongue: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "paperScraps", value: 5 } }]
- Irresistible Charm: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "dungeon_crawl", encounterType: "any" }, modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "auto_complete_encounter" }, description: "Auto-complete any encounter with same book as dungeon room" }]
- Empowered Bond: [{ trigger: "ON_STATE_LOAD", modifier: { type: "MULTIPLY", target: "familiar_bonus", value: 2 }, description: "Familiar equipped bonuses increased by +5" }]
- Echo Chamber: [{ trigger: "ON_STATE_LOAD", modifier: { type: "MULTIPLY", target: "familiar_bonuses", value: 2 }, description: "All familiar bonuses doubled" }]
- Alchemic Focus: [{ trigger: "ON_QUEST_COMPLETED", condition: { questType: "extra_credit" }, modifier: { type: "ADD_FLAT", resource: "xp", value: 5 } }]
- Philosopher's Stone: [{ trigger: "ON_ACTIVATE", modifier: { type: "ACTIVATE", action: "sacrifice_xp_for_currency" }, cooldown: "monthly" }]

### 6. Update generate-data.js and data.js
Ensure generate-data.js exports the new effects fields. In data.js, ensure the new effects are accessible.

### 7. Tests
Create tests/ModifierPipeline.test.js:
- Test resolve() with ADD_FLAT effects
- Test resolve() with MULTIPLY effects (applied after flat)
- Test resolve() with mixed flat + multiplier
- Test condition evaluation (questType, genre, pageCount, etc.)
- Test that effects with unmet conditions are skipped
- Test receipt tracking
- Test EffectRegistry gathers correct active effects for a given trigger

Run ALL existing tests and verify they still pass (this phase has NO behavior changes).

## Key Files
- NEW: assets/js/services/effectSchema.js
- NEW: assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js
- NEW: assets/js/services/EffectRegistry.js
- NEW: assets/js/services/TriggerPayload.js
- NEW: tests/ModifierPipeline.test.js
- MODIFIED: assets/data/keeperBackgrounds.json
- MODIFIED: assets/data/schoolBenefits.json
- MODIFIED: assets/data/masteryAbilities.json
- MODIFIED: scripts/generate-data.js (ensure effects exported)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet/data.js (ensure effects accessible)

## Success Criteria
- All new services created with clean APIs
- All JSON files have effects arrays with valid effect objects
- ModifierPipeline.test.js passes
- ALL existing tests still pass (npm test in tests/ directory)
- No behavior changes to the running application

Phase 5: Wire permanent effects (ON_STATE_LOAD) and activated abilities (ON_ACTIVATE)
description:
## Goal
Wire the ModifierPipeline for permanent effects that apply on game load and activated abilities that players manually trigger with cooldowns.

## Context
Some effects are always active (modify game state permanently) and some are one-click abilities with monthly cooldowns. Currently these are either not implemented or require manual player tracking.

## Permanent Effects (ON_STATE_LOAD)
These fire when the game loads or when equipment/class changes:

### Conjuration school: Extra familiar slot
- Effect: { trigger: ON_STATE_LOAD, modifier: { type: UNLOCK_SLOT, slotType: "familiar" } }
- Currently: schoolBenefits.json says "When you complete a quest while your Familiar slot is equipped, you gain an additional +5 Ink Drops for each quest" but the description being updated to "Gain an additional familiar slot"
- Implementation: When state loads or school changes, EffectRegistry checks for UNLOCK_SLOT effects and applies them. SlotService.js should query the registry for extra slots.

### Echo Chamber (mastery, Conjuration cost 2): Double familiar bonuses
- Effect: { trigger: ON_STATE_LOAD, modifier: { type: MULTIPLY, target: "familiar_bonuses", value: 2 } }
- New text: "The bonuses granted by each familiar you have equipped are doubled"
- Implementation: When calculating item modifiers in the pipeline, if Echo Chamber is learned, familiar item rewardModifier values are doubled before applying. This requires the pipeline to recognize familiar-type items and apply the Echo Chamber multiplier to their bonus values.

### Empowered Bond (mastery, Conjuration cost 1): +5 to familiar bonus
- Effect: { trigger: ON_STATE_LOAD, modifier: { type: ADD_FLAT, target: "familiar_bonus", value: 5 } }
- Current text: "Familiar Ink Drop or Paper Scrap bonus permanently increased by +5"
- Implementation: Similar to Echo Chamber but adds flat +5 to the primary resource of each familiar's bonus.

### Transmutation school: UI unlock
- Effect: { trigger: ON_STATE_LOAD, modifier: { type: UNLOCK_SLOT, slotType: "transmutation_ui" } }
- Current text: "Once per month, you may transmute your currency"
- Implementation: Shows/hides the transmutation UI section based on school selection.

## Activated Abilities (ON_ACTIVATE)
These are abilities the player actively triggers, with monthly cooldowns. They need:
1. A UI button/action to trigger them
2. Cooldown tracking (monthly reset at EOM)
3. Clear feedback when activated and when on cooldown

### Evocation school: Complete two quests with one book
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "complete_two_quests_one_book" }, cooldown: "monthly" }
- New text: "Once per month you may use one book to complete two quests, regardless of prompt"
- UI: Button in quest tab that marks two active quests as completed using the same book

### Scepter of Knowledge (item): Ignore prompt for non-fiction
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "ignore_prompt_read_nonfiction" }, cooldown: "monthly" }
- New text: "Once per month you may ignore the prompt and read a non-fiction book instead"
- UI: Button or checkbox when completing a quest

### Philosopher's Stone (mastery, Transmutation cost 2): Sacrifice XP for currency
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "sacrifice_xp_for_currency" }, cooldown: "monthly" }
- Current text: "Sacrifice 50 XP to instantly gain 50 Ink Drops and 10 Paper Scraps"
- UI: Button in transmutation UI section

### Irresistible Charm (mastery, Enchantment cost 2): Auto-complete encounter
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "auto_complete_encounter" }, cooldown: "monthly" }
- New text: "You can choose to automatically complete any encounter prompt with the same book as the dungeon room"
- UI: Button when in dungeon encounter quest

### Quick Shot (mastery, Evocation cost 1): Same book for room + encounter
- Not exactly a cooldown ability — it's a permanent passive that changes dungeon rules
- New text: "Any dungeon room and encounter can be completed by the same book, regardless of prompt"
- Implementation: When Quick Shot is learned, dungeon quest validation allows same book for room and encounter prompts

### Concussive Blast (mastery, Evocation cost 2): Complete adjacent quest
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "complete_adjacent_quest" } }
- New text: "When completing a quest you may choose to also complete the quest to the left or right of the quest card with the same book"
- UI: After completing a quest, show option to also complete an adjacent one

### Grand Dispelling (mastery, Abjuration cost 2): Remove all worn pages
- Effect: { trigger: ON_ACTIVATE, modifier: { type: ACTIVATE, action: "remove_all_worn_pages" }, cooldown: "monthly" }
- UI: Button in curse tab

## Cooldown System
Add to state/stateAdapter:
- New storage key: ABILITY_COOLDOWNS — object mapping effectId → { lastMonth, lastYear }
- StateAdapter methods: consumeCooldown(effectId, month, year), isCooldownAvailable(effectId, month, year), resetMonthlyCooldowns()
- EndOfMonthController resets monthly cooldowns

## Key Files
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/ModifierPipeline.js (UNLOCK_SLOT, ACTIVATE resolution)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/EffectRegistry.js (permanent effect detection, cooldown checks)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/InventoryController.js (extra familiar slot from Conjuration/Echo Chamber)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/QuestController.js (activated abilities UI)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/CurseController.js (Grand Dispelling button)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/CharacterController.js (ON_STATE_LOAD effects when school/class changes)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/services/SlotService.js (query registry for extra slots)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet/storageKeys.js (ABILITY_COOLDOWNS key)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/character-sheet/stateAdapter.js (cooldown methods)
- MODIFIED: assets/js/controllers/EndOfMonthController.js (reset cooldowns)

## Success Criteria
- Conjuration school auto-unlocks extra familiar slot
- Echo Chamber doubles familiar bonuses when learned
- Empowered Bond adds +5 to familiar bonus when learned
- Activated abilities have working UI with cooldown tracking
- Monthly cooldowns reset at end of month
- All existing tests pass
